Platform & Data.narrative: This family ensures your finance system is accessible, secure, and connected to the rest of your business. Singapore SMEs must comply with PDPA data protection rules and need staff to access financial data safely from anywhere—especially in hybrid work environments. It matters when your accountant needs to approve an invoice from a client site, or when you're integrating bank feeds and tax filings with ACRA.

Core Ledger & Close.narrative: This is the backbone of financial accounting—it records every transaction, tracks your assets, and produces the profit-and-loss and balance-sheet statements you need for tax and lending decisions. Every Singapore SME must close its books monthly and annually, and auditors will expect a clean audit trail and proper depreciation records. It matters when you're preparing year-end accounts for ACRA filing or explaining your financial position to a bank for a loan.

Revenue Cycle.narrative: This family handles customer invoicing, payment collection, and credit management—the lifeblood of cash flow. Singapore SMEs need to issue invoices quickly, track who owes money, and integrate with local payment schemes (PayNow, GIRO) and e-invoicing standards (InvoiceNow) to stay competitive and compliant. It matters when you're chasing overdue invoices or accepting online payments from customers without manual bank transfers.

Expense Cycle.narrative: This family captures supplier bills, employee expenses, and purchase orders—ensuring you pay the right amount to the right vendor on time and track project costs accurately. Singapore SMEs benefit from automating expense claims (especially corporate-card feeds) and matching invoices to purchase orders to prevent overpayment and fraud. It matters when your team submits travel claims or you need to know the true cost of a project before quoting to a customer.

Treasury & Compliance.narrative: This family manages your cash, tax obligations, and regulatory filings—critical for survival and legal compliance in Singapore. You must reconcile bank accounts, calculate and file GST returns (F5 form), and handle multi-currency transactions if you trade internationally. It matters when you're forecasting cash to avoid a shortfall, filing your quarterly GST return with ACRA, or receiving payment in USD from an overseas customer.

Planning & Analytics.narrative: This family helps you plan ahead with budgets and dashboards, and spot trends or anomalies in your finances using AI. Singapore SMEs use budgeting to control spending and dashboards to see at a glance whether they're on track—especially useful for fast-growing businesses or those managing multiple projects. It matters when you're setting next year's budget with your team or your CFO wants to spot a sudden spike in supplier costs.
